control_map_artifact.visual_assessment: Depth diagnostic preserves coherent foreground/background separation and the same portrait silhouette as the prepared DepthAnything map. Face/hair/shoulder mass is smooth as expected for depth conditioning.

qa_findings: Generated image is a coherent hyperrealistic head-and-shoulders portrait with readable eyes, believable hair, realistic blazer/shirt clothing, and depth-separated background.
Depth conditioning did not collapse the face or create obvious depth-map stains.
Image has low-key exposure with slightly shadowed eyes and cheeks; acceptable for local smoke, not enough for final certification.
Skin is slightly polished/beauty-smoothed, similar to earlier local Canny notes.
Dark out-of-focus foreground/background shapes at upper right are coherent enough for smoke but should be watched in a follow-up depth prompt if this lane is promoted.
remaining_risks: Single local smoke image is not full depth lane certification.
Hands, full-body anatomy, contact points, and multi-seed robustness are not certified because they are not visible or not sampled.
Target-runtime EC2 object_info/path/hash/input proof, bounded generation, pullback, technical QA, and strict whole-image visual QA remain separate gates.
next_action: Update lane state and blockers to show the depth lane is locally smoke-proven with notes, then run a QA-driven follow-up only if depth-specific defects need iteration.
